Retry auto-connect on first user tap if page load attempt failed

Chrome blocks getDevices()+gatt.connect() without user interaction.
Retrying on first pointerdown guarantees it fires after a gesture.

Fix TDZ error — move unresolvedJobs declaration above report insert

unresolvedJobs was declared with let at line 74 but referenced at
line 64 inside the report insert. JavaScript temporal dead zone
caused 'Cannot access y before initialization' on every end-shift
call. Move the declaration and at_risk snapshot query above the
report insert so the variable exists when the insert reads it.

chore: gitignore claude artifacts and uv.lock

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Sanitize postShiftChecks before JSON.stringify + add end-shift debug log

Force every postShiftChecks value to a plain boolean via !!v before
it reaches JSON.stringify in the fetch body and localStorage summary.
If a DOM element or event object leaked into the state, this prevents
"Converting circular structure to JSON" from silently killing the
end-shift POST. Also add a console.log before the driverInfo guard
in submitEndShift so we can verify the fetch preconditions in devtools.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: remove unused import flagged by ruff

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(DFA): relocate Q1/R24 right of J3 to clear battery cable path

User feedback: Q1 and R24 positioned above J3 body blocked the
battery cable exit path. Relocated to the right side:

  Q1: (80, 52.5) → (85, 53) — right-above J3.4 mech tab
  R24: (83.5, 53.5) → (86, 56) — below Q1, between J3 tabs and GND

BAT+ route: approach via (80.01) → horizontal at y=52.5 to x=85
→ Q1 drain. BAT_IN: Q1 source → horizontal at y=54.1 to x=79 →
J3.1. RPP_GATE: Q1 gate → L-shape to R24.1. GND: R24.2 → via.

Also fixed R24 _compute_pads rotation mismatch (rot=0 vs board
rot=180) that caused _pad() to return swapped positions.

Gate suite: trace_through_pad PASS, trace_crossings PASS,
copper_clearance 0 DANGER, DFM 119/119, net_connectivity PASS,
design_intent 364/364, datasheet_nets ALL PASS.

commit.message:
ci: add quality workflow (pytest, vitest, ruff, black, mypy, eslint, prettier, gitleaks, tfsec)

.github/workflows/quality.yaml runs on every push to main and every
pull request. Three parallel jobs:

python:
  - Set up Python 3.12 with pip cache
  - Install app/requirements.txt
  - ruff check, black --check, mypy, pytest -q

typescript:
  - Set up Node 20 with npm cache
  - npm install in frontend/
  - tsc --noEmit, eslint, prettier --check, vitest run

secret-scan:
  - gitleaks (uses .gitleaks.toml custom rules)
  - tfsec on infra/terraform/

This backstops the pre-commit hook and is the first place pytest +
vitest actually run in the project. The deploy workflows (step 5)
will live alongside this one without overlap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

that production apps can use for email-based passwordless login.

commit.message:
🔌 Add @umpire/eslint-plugin (#21)

* 🔌 Add @umpire/eslint-plugin with three initial rules

Adds `packages/eslint-plugin` as a new published workspace package.

Rules:
- `no-unknown-fields` — reports field-name string literals in rule
  factories (requires, disables, oneOf, anyOf, eitherOf, enabledWhen,
  fairWhen) that are not declared in the same umpire() fields config.
  Handles the check() helper in disables source position and recurses
  into anyOf/eitherOf. Bails out silently when fields contains spread
  elements to avoid false positives.
- `no-inline-umpire-init` — reports umpire() calls inside React
  component or hook bodies (name starts with uppercase or `use`) that
  are not wrapped in useMemo. Building the dependency graph on every
  render is an invisible perf regression with no runtime error.
- `no-self-disable` — reports when the source field of disables() also
  appears in its targets array.

Exports a `recommended` flat-config preset that sets no-unknown-fields
and no-inline-umpire-init to warn, and no-self-disable to error.

36 tests pass across all three rules.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016ZuZgFVvc37SSn1bKDPW8b

* 🔍 Add no-contradicting-rules and no-circular-requires

Both rules operate within a single umpire() call's top-level rules array
and skip rules nested inside anyOf/eitherOf (OR/branch semantics).

no-contradicting-rules (error):
  Detects requires/disables pairs that make a field permanently unavailable.
  Two patterns:
  - Case A: requires(X, Y) + disables(Y, [X]) — Y satisfies, disables X,
    but X needs Y → X can never be enabled
  - Case B: requires(X, Y) + disables(X, [Y]) — X satisfies, disables Y,
    but X needs Y → X's requirement can never hold while X has a value
  Reports on the disables() call with field names in the message.

no-circular-requires (error):
  Detects cycles in the requires dependency graph using DFS with gray/black
  coloring. Handles cycles of any length (A↔B, A→B→C→A, self-cycles).
  Reports on the requires() call whose dep edge closes the cycle, with the
  full cycle path in the message (e.g. 'a' → 'b' → 'a').
  Deduplicates cycles normalized by rotating to the lexicographically
  smallest node, so A→B→A and B→A→B don't report twice.

Both rules are included in the recommended config at error severity.
55 tests pass across 5 files.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016ZuZgFVvc37SSn1bKDPW8b

  setup, per-rule bad/good examples, link back from api/rules

commit.message:
fix(agent): use result.output not result.data (Pydantic AI 1.79 API)

AgentRunResult in pydantic-ai 1.79 exposes .output, not .data.
The dispatch was hitting the catch-all error handler because of
AttributeError on every agent call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: enable Better Auth cookieCache to eliminate per-request DB session lookups

Without cookieCache, every page navigation triggers a DB query via the middleware's
/api/auth/get-session call. With cookieCache enabled, the session payload is stored
in a signed encrypted cookie and verified locally for up to 300s — zero DB hits.

Also lowered the rate limit from 200/min to 50/min now that middleware no longer
hammers the endpoint on every navigation.

Trade-off: deactivated users can act for up to 5 min before forced out.
Acceptable — session revocation (T01R-R1) handles the critical path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: enable CUDA on Linux without feature flag, fix provider detection

CUDA EP was gated behind #[cfg(feature = "cuda")] which required
a crate-level feature flag that was never enabled in release builds.
Changed to #[cfg(not(target_os = "macos"))] — CUDA EP is always
registered on Linux/Windows, ORT silently falls back to CPU if
CUDA drivers aren't available.

Also fixed detect_active_provider() to report platform-appropriate
provider (CUDA on Linux, DirectML on Windows, CoreML on macOS)
without requiring compile-time feature flags.

macOS builds unaffected — CoreML was already platform-gated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add UniG2U benchmark task with model support (#1297)

* Add UniG2U benchmark task with model support

Add UniG2U (Unified Generation-to-Understanding) benchmark covering
11 sub-tasks across chart understanding, geometry, physics, spatial
planning, and visual puzzles.

Two evaluation modes:
- unig2u: standard understanding (single-stage)
- unig2u_GtA: Visual CoT two-stage (generate auxiliary image, then answer)

GtA triggered explicitly via generation_kwargs.visual_cot: true.

New model implementations: Ovis-U1, ILLUME+, MMaDa, Qwen-Image-Edit.

via [HAPI](https://hapi.run)

Co-Authored-By: HAPI <noreply@hapi.run>

* Fix qwen_image_edit device_map for diffusion pipeline

DiffusionPipeline does not support device_map="auto", use "balanced" instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Visual CoT (GtA) support check for models

Models without GtA implementation now raise a clear error when run on
visual_cot tasks, instead of silently degrading with garbled prompts.

- Add `supports_visual_cot` class attribute to lmms base (default False)
- Add `_check_visual_cot_support()` guard in base class
- Call the check in evaluator before generate_until
- Set `supports_visual_cot = True` on ovis_u1, bagel_unig2u, illume_plus, qwen_image_edit

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert "Add Visual CoT (GtA) support check for models"

This reverts commit 2bc4f01b5daf31a891412fea57caa69d86fe8c28.

* Add generate_visual_cot as dedicated output type for GtA tasks

Visual CoT (GtA) tasks now use output_type: generate_visual_cot instead
of generate_until. Models must implement generate_visual_cot() to run
these tasks — models without it get a clear NotImplementedError.

- Add generate_visual_cot() default in lmms base class (raises NotImplementedError)
- Register generate_visual_cot in ALL_OUTPUT_TYPES and construct_requests
- All 31 visual_cot yaml configs: output_type → generate_visual_cot
- 4 GtA models (ovis_u1, bagel_unig2u, illume_plus, qwen_image_edit):
  implement generate_visual_cot() delegating to generate_until()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reflect the new GtA mechanism: models must implement
generate_visual_cot() to run visual_cot tasks.

feat(cli): load .env file in dazzle serve --local (#769)

Adds a stdlib-only `_load_dotenv` helper to serve.py that reads
<project_root>/.env before _validate_infrastructure() is called.
Existing os.environ values take precedence (setdefault semantics),
so shell exports still override .env.

Supports:
- KEY=VALUE lines
- export KEY=VALUE syntax
- # comment lines
- Blank lines
- Quoted values ("..." and '...')
- Missing .env file (no-op)

This closes #769 and partially addresses #768 (the .env loading
step of frictionless spinup). A --dev-seed flag for user/tenant
creation is a separate follow-up.

No new dependencies — python-dotenv not needed.

Closes #769.

commit.message:
fix(T037): add mark-ready-for-payment button to cashier dashboard and stylist/secretary views

transitionToAwaitingPayment was implemented in the server action (T037) but
never wired to any UI. Adds a "Listo para cobrar" button:
- Cashier dashboard: on every logged/reopened ticket card (cashier can act on any)
- Stylist/secretary home: on their own logged ticket items via MyEditRequests

Also adds ticketStatus to OpenTicketItem type so the status is available

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test(dashboard): add integration gates for real window pointer events (#770)

Adds TestDashboardIntegrationGates class with 4 tests that dispatch real
DOM PointerEvent objects via window.dispatchEvent() and verify the
@pointermove.window / @pointerup.window handlers are wired correctly.

These complement the unit gates (which test controller state transitions
in isolation) and catch the specific class of bug #770 identified: the
setPointerCapture bug redirected pointermove events away from the window
listener, which unit tests couldn't catch because they called methods
directly with mock event objects.

Approach: seed drag state via startDrag() method call (bypasses #771 —
@pointerdown binding inside <template x-for>), then fire real window
pointer events and verify state transitions. This tests the exact
pipeline #770 broke (pointermove.window / pointerup.window routing),
which was the actual regression vector.

Closes #770.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Use acceptAllDevices to show all BLE devices in chooser

Name filter was hiding ELK-BLEDOM from Chrome's chooser.
Now shows all nearby BLE devices so user can pick manually.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Navbar stale auth state after sign-in/sign-out

Two race conditions caused the Navbar to show the wrong auth state:

1. auth-callback navigated via router.replace() without router.refresh(),
   so Next.js served cached RSC payloads rendered before authentication.
   Added router.refresh() to invalidate the cache before navigating.

2. Sign-out used await signOut() + window.location.assign("/"), but the
   navigation could fire before Clerk fully cleared the session cookie.
   Switched to signOut({ redirectUrl }) which lets Clerk handle timing.

Also added NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_IN_URL and SIGN_UP_URL env vars
locally — without these Clerk partially redirects to its hosted Account
Portal instead of the custom pages, causing multiple login windows.
These must also be set in Vercel env vars for production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(scripts): install-splunk.sh now installs cloudflared

After Splunk install + HEC enable, fetch the latest cloudflared deb
from GitHub releases, install it, and register it as a systemd
service using the operator-supplied CLOUDFLARED_TOKEN env var.

- Idempotent: skips install if cloudflared is already running.
- Token source: operator pulls it from Key Vault via `az keyvault
  secret show --name cloudflare-tunnel-splunk-token`.
- Output messages now point at the Zero Trust dashboard instead of
  a public http://<vm-ip>:8000 URL (that NSG rule is gone).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(infra): add Cloudflare tunnel token secrets; drop Splunk 8000 NSG rule

Layer 8 (Cloudflare Tunnel + Zero Trust) lands in Terraform. This
commit adds only what infra needs: two KV secret shells for the
connector tokens and an NSG cleanup.

keyvault.tf:
- Add cloudflare-tunnel-splunk-token secret shell (lifecycle.ignore)
- Add cloudflare-tunnel-chatbot-token secret shell (lifecycle.ignore)
- Both set-me-in-portal, depend on the operator access policy

splunk-vm.tf:
- Remove the allow-splunk-web-operator NSG rule. Port 8000 is no longer
  exposed inbound — cloudflared handles reachability via outbound
  tunnel dial. Splunk VM's public IP stays for SSH only.

Pre-commit (gitleaks + tfsec): all hooks passed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: promote Cloudflare Tunnel + Zero Trust from v2 to v1

Pivot from the deferred "v2" draft to a shipping "v1" spec. Cloudflare
Zero Trust Free is now live on the project's Cloudflare account with
team domain money-honey.cloudflareaccess.com and two named tunnels
(money-honey-splunk and money-honey-chatbot) created in the dashboard.

Changes:
- Rename docs/specs/cloudflare-access-v2.md to cloudflare-access-v1.md
  and rewrite for the v1 reality: Terraform only manages the two KV
  token secrets; tunnel + Access config is manual in the dashboard.
- infra-v1.md OS-11: flipped from "deferred to v2" to "moved into v1".
- CLAUDE.md: add Layer 8 "Identity-gated edge" describing cloudflared
  on each origin with tokens sourced from Key Vault. Header updated to
  "Three Domains, Eight Layers". Cloudflare entry removed from
  Deferred to v2.

No code changes in this commit — Terraform and install script updates
come in commits 2 and 3.
